summary: Tested new v6 features: Cuckoo sandbox, VNS alerts, quantum security, PDF reporting, and Tactical Heatmap. All 8 backend tests passed. Frontend Tactical Heatmap page fully functional with heat spots, stats cards, and threat type analysis. Navigation link works correctly.

issues: Console warnings about chart width/height being -1 - minor recharts sizing issue during initial render

passed_tests: GET /api/advanced/sandbox/status - Returns sandbox status (mode: static_analysis when Cuckoo not configured)
GET /api/advanced/alerts/status - Returns VNS alert service status
GET /api/advanced/quantum/status - Shows mode (simulation) and algorithms (KYBER, DILITHIUM)
GET /api/threats - Returns threat data for heatmap visualization
Frontend Tactical Heatmap page loads with title 'Tactical Threat Heatmap'
Frontend heatmap canvas renders at 1566x500 with heat spots
Frontend stat cards display: Total (23), Critical (9), High (13), Medium (1), Low (0)
Frontend Threat Type Analysis section displays threat breakdown
Frontend sidebar navigation link 'Threat Heatmap' navigates to /heatmap
Frontend Advanced Services page still functional with all 6 tabs

alerts_configure.note: Successfully configured Slack webhook - slack_configured becomes true

navigation.sidebar_link: Threat Heatmap link visible and clickable

seed_data_creation: Used existing threat data (23 threats) for heatmap visualization
context_for_next_testing_agent: All v6 features working: 1) Cuckoo sandbox runs in static_analysis mode (no Cuckoo API configured). 2) VNS alerts service ready but no channels configured by default - can configure via /api/advanced/alerts/configure. 3) Quantum security in simulation mode - liboqs required for production. 4) PDF reports generate successfully with charts. 5) Tactical Heatmap page fully functional at /heatmap with heat visualization by threat type and severity breakdown.

mocked_apis_list: Cuckoo sandbox uses static analysis fallback (no CUCKOO_API_URL configured)
Quantum crypto in simulation mode (liboqs not installed)
VNS alerts disabled by default (no Slack webhook or SMTP configured)
